title: HTTP Client Ownership, Context, Status, Body Limits, and Cleanup · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A regional field-research team incrementally builds reusable bounded context-aware PokéAPI client from original fixed PokéAPI fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP, Git, quality-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of reusable bounded context-aware PokéAPI client while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Inject and reuse one configured http.Client or narrow transport port because transports retain connection state and support concurrent use.
Create requests with caller-owned context and derive request deadlines without hiding cancellation behind Background.
Classify success, not found, client, server, redirect, and unexpected status before domain decoding and retain bounded error evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go Client Reuse
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 4955 during a disconnected community-lab recovery drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove client reuse, response admission, bounded bodies, context cancellation, and cleanup; no user-visible meaning may rely on color or cursor motion; then change one resource identity, pagination link, JSON field, status, or body budget and revalidate admission. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject and reuse one configured http.Client or narrow transport port because transports retain connection state and support concurrent use.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go Client Reuse.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inject and reuse one configured http.client or narrow transport port because transports retain connection state and support concurrent use. For Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go Client Reuse, keep Pokedex Go Client Reuse connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Pokedex Go Roundtrippe…
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-go-roundtripper-tests-pkmyhq" that uses case 9585 to prove client reuse, response admission, bounded bodies, context cancellation, and cleanup. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact PokéAPI, read or write host files, handle real terminal or OS signals, spawn unbounded work, or claim race, leak, load, accessibility, cross-platform, package, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, race and fuzz runs, temporary files, subprocess and signal harnesses, inspected artifacts, representative platforms, accessibility review, capacity, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Pokedex product engineering and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Pokedex Go Roundtrippe…, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Pokedex Go Roundtripper Tests precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Pokedex product engineering and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Pokedex Go Roundtrippe…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A workshop Go team handles case 1024 during an accessible regional field-guide session. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove client reuse, response admission, bounded bodies, context cancellation, and cleanup; all goroutines, bodies, signals, timers, and files require named cleanup ownership; then change one command, argument, EOF, or output-width condition and preserve the session contract. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use injected RoundTripper and httptest server cases for headers, URL, context, status, body limit, cancellation, and cleanup.

For Pokedex product engineering and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Pokedex Go Roundtrippe…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Testing only the returned Pokemon proves request construction and lifecycle.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Pokedex Go Roundtripper T…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 2643 during a clean cross-platform package smoke. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove client reuse, response admission, bounded bodies, context cancellation, and cleanup; all goroutines, bodies, signals, timers, and files require named cleanup ownership; then change one toolchain, target, artifact, migration, rollback, or restore condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use injected RoundTripper and httptest server cases for headers, URL, context, status, body limit, cancellation, and cleanup.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: Pokedex product engineering and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Pokedex Go Roundtripper T….

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Pokedex Go Roundtripper Tests symptom.

stimulus.title: Pokedex Go Roundtripper Tests failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Testing only the returned Pokemon proves request construction and lifecycle.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use injected RoundTripper and httptest server cases for headers, URL, context, status, body limit, cancellation, and cleanup.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Pokedex Go Roundtripper Tests happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Pokedex Go Roundtripper Tests condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Defend the transferred model · Workshop Pokedex Go Roundtripper…
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Pokedex Go Roundtripper Tests: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop Go team handles case 4262 during a narrow-terminal ranger workflow. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove client reuse, response admission, bounded bodies, context cancellation, and cleanup; the fixed PokéAPI origin and fair-use traffic budget must remain enforced; then inject one redirect, control sequence, file, timeout, or persistence failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use injected RoundTripper and httptest server cases for headers, URL, context, status, body limit, cancellation, and cleanup.

Evidence target: Pokedex product engineering and Go Defend the transferred model · Workshop Pokedex Go Roundtripper….
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Pokedex product engineering and Go Defend the transferred model · Workshop Pokedex Go Roundtripper…, keep Pokedex Go Roundtripper Tests connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use injected RoundTripper and httptest server cases for headers, URL, context, status, body limit, cancellation, and cleanup.
